sections.heading: Core Principle
sections.text: The project treats retrieval quality as the primary determinant of dossier-review quality. Model choice is secondary to chunking, source coverage, query decomposition, and evidence reconciliation.

sections.heading: Query Decomposition
sections.text: Complex reviewer questions should be decomposed into focused sub-queries before retrieval. Each sub-query targets a specific document, section, or external knowledge topic, and the results are merged with explicit provenance.

sections.heading: Evaluation Focus
sections.text: Retrieval quality must be measured independently from answer generation using recall, ranking quality, multi-hop coverage, and source-attribution completeness.

title: WHO AWaRe and GLASS Policy Stack

sections.heading: AWaRe Source of Truth
sections.text: WHO AWaRe is the authoritative source for Access, Watch, and Reserve assignment. Production policy decisions must not rely on synthetic or hand-maintained substitute lists.

sections.heading: Reserve Stewardship
sections.text: Reserve antibiotics can be accelerated for review when they address critical MDR unmet need, but authorization controls should remain restrictive to preserve last-resort effectiveness.

sections.heading: Watch Escalation
sections.text: A Watch antibiotic that is highly similar to an existing Watch comparator and paired with rising GLASS resistance should be escalated for restricted authorization and deeper review.

sections.heading: Normalization Order
sections.text: Ingredient names should be normalized through RxNorm before chemistry comparison. Brand names, salts, hydrates, and formulation-level strings must resolve to an active moiety or parent ingredient.

sections.heading: Primary Chemistry Source
sections.text: PubChem PUG-REST is the primary source for canonical SMILES, InChI, InChIKey, parent-compound relationships, and structure-level lookup.

sections.heading: Cross-Check and Reconciliation
sections.text: ChEMBL and UniChem should be used to cross-check identifiers and chemistry context. If the normalization chain cannot reconcile identifiers confidently, the policy flow should abstain rather than infer.

sections.heading: Role of the Model Switch
sections.text: UI model switching exists to let reviewers and developers compare local synthesis behavior across Gemma E4B, Gemma E2B, and Qwen 3.5. It does not replace retrieval quality or source validation.

sections.heading: Recommended Usage
sections.text: Gemma E4B should be used as the balanced default, Gemma E2B as the lighter fast option, and Qwen 3.5 as an alternative synthesis profile for difficult comparative questions.

sections.heading: Audit Requirement
sections.text: Every review response and audit trace should record the selected model profile so differences in synthesis can be reproduced.
